seoTitle: Injoinic Products - Fast Charging & Power Management ICs Distributor
seoDescription: Explore Injoinic's comprehensive product portfolio including fast charging protocol ICs, power management chips, wireless charging solutions, and battery management ICs. LiTong is your authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What product categories does Injoinic offer?
faqs.answer: Injoinic offers four main product categories: Fast Charging Protocol ICs supporting PD, QC, SCP, FCP and other standards; Power Management ICs including DC-DC converters, LDOs, and PMICs; Wireless Charging ICs for Qi-compatible solutions; and Battery Management ICs providing protection and monitoring for lithium batteries. Each category includes multiple product series targeting different application requirements and performance levels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right solution for your application. Contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Injoinic product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Injoinic product involves several steps. First, identify your application requirements including input/output voltage, current capacity, and supported protocols. Second, determine the charging standards you need to support (PD, QC, SCP, etc.). Third, consider integration level and external component requirements. Fourth, evaluate thermal performance and efficiency requirements. Fifth, check package options for your PCB layout. Injoinic provides selection guides and reference designs to help with the decision process. Our FAE team can also provide personalized recommendations based on your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our selection guides or contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between Injoinic's fast charging protocol ICs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic's fast charging protocol ICs differ in supported protocols, integration level, and target applications. Some models focus on single-protocol solutions for cost-sensitive applications, while others support multi-protocol for universal compatibility. Higher-end models integrate additional features such as cable compensation, temperature monitoring, and advanced protection. The IP series targets power bank applications with specific optimizations for battery charging and discharging. The IU series focuses on adapter and charger applications with high-efficiency conversion. Understanding these differences helps select the optimal solution for your specific use case.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare protocol support and integration features to select the right IC. Contact us for detailed comparison and recommendations.

faqs.question: Which Injoinic products are suitable for power bank applications?
faqs.answer: Injoinic offers several product series specifically designed for power bank applications. The IP series protocol ICs provide comprehensive solutions supporting multiple fast charging protocols for both input and output. These chips integrate battery management functions, power path control, and protection features. For power conversion, Injoinic's DC-DC converters offer high-efficiency synchronous buck-boost operation. The combination of protocol IC and power management IC creates a complete power bank solution with minimal external components. Reference designs are available for various power bank configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For power bank designs, consider the IP series protocol ICs combined with appropriate power management solutions. Request reference designs from our team.

faqs.question: What wireless charging solutions does Injoinic provide?
faqs.answer: Injoinic provides wireless charging ICs compliant with the Qi standard for both transmitter and receiver applications. Their solutions support various power levels from 5W basic charging to 15W fast wireless charging. The ICs integrate full-bridge drivers, power control, and communication protocols, minimizing external component count. Features include foreign object detection (FOD), temperature monitoring, and comprehensive protection functions. Injoinic's wireless charging solutions are suitable for charging pads, stands, and integration into devices such as smartphones and TWS earphone cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: For wireless charging applications, select based on required power level and features. Contact us for transmitter and receiver solution recommendations.

categories.name: Fast Charging Protocol ICs
categories.description: Comprehensive fast charging protocol chips supporting all major standards including USB PD, QC, SCP, FCP, and more for universal charging compatibility.
categories.longDescription: Injoinic's fast charging protocol ICs provide comprehensive solutions for modern charging applications. These highly integrated chips support all major fast charging standards including USB Power Delivery (PD), Qualcomm Quick Charge (QC), Huawei SuperCharge (SCP), Samsung AFC, and more. The protocol ICs handle automatic protocol detection and negotiation, enabling universal compatibility with devices from all major manufacturers. With features like cable compensation, temperature monitoring, and advanced protection, these ICs simplify charger and power bank design while ensuring safe and efficient charging. The high integration level reduces BOM cost and PCB space requirements.

series.name: IP Series
series.description: Multi-protocol fast charging ICs for power banks and adapters

series.name: IU Series
series.description: High-efficiency protocol ICs for wall chargers and car chargers

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Fast Charging Protocol ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider supported protocols, power level, and integration requirements

faqs.question: What protocols does the IP2723T support?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T supports comprehensive fast charging protocols including USB PD 3.0/3.1 with PPS, Qualcomm QC 2.0/3.0/4.0+, Huawei SCP and FCP, Samsung AFC, and MediaTek PE. This multi-protocol support enables universal compatibility with smartphones, tablets, and laptops from all major manufacturers. The IC automatically detects the connected device's protocol and negotiates optimal charging parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IP2723T for multi-protocol applications requiring broad device compatibility. Contact us for samples and reference designs.

faqs.question: How do I configure the output voltage and current?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T supports flexible output configuration through external resistors or I2C interface. For resistor configuration, connect appropriate resistors to the CFG pins to set default voltage and current limits. For advanced applications, use the I2C interface to dynamically adjust output parameters based on system conditions. The IC also supports cable compensation to maintain stable voltage at the device end. Refer to the datasheet for detailed configuration options and calculation formulas.
faqs.decisionGuide: For simple applications, use resistor configuration. For dynamic control, implement I2C communication. Our FAE team can provide configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What protection features are integrated in Injoinic protocol ICs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic protocol ICs integrate comprehensive protection features including over-voltage protection (OVP), under-voltage protection (UVP), over-current protection (OCP), short-circuit protection (SCP), and over-temperature protection (OTP). These protections ensure safe operation under abnormal conditions and protect both the charger and connected devices. The ICs also feature ESD protection on all pins and robust fault handling mechanisms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Injoinic's integrated protections simplify design and improve reliability. Review the datasheet for specific protection thresholds and characteristics.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IP and IU series?
faqs.answer: The IP series is optimized for power bank applications with features like battery management integration, power path control, and bidirectional charging support. The IU series targets adapter and charger applications with focus on high-efficiency AC-DC conversion and compact designs. IP series typically includes more integrated functions for battery-powered applications, while IU series emphasizes high power density and thermal performance for wall adapters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select IP series for power banks and portable applications. Choose IU series for wall chargers and adapters. Contact us for specific model recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I implement PPS (Programmable Power Supply) with Injoinic ICs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic's PD 3.1 compatible ICs support PPS for precise voltage and current control. The IC handles PPS communication with the connected device and adjusts output accordingly. Implementing PPS requires proper firmware support and may need external MCU for advanced applications. The IC provides feedback signals for output adjustment and reports actual output parameters to the source device. PPS enables optimized charging for newer smartphones and laptops.
faqs.decisionGuide: For PPS support, select PD 3.1 compatible models. Contact our FAE team for implementation guidance and reference code.

products.name: Multi-Protocol Fast Charging Controller
products.shortDescription: Highly integrated multi-protocol fast charging IC supporting PD 3.1, QC 4.0+, SCP, FCP with automatic protocol detect...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP2723T is a highly integrated multi-protocol fast charging controller designed for modern charging applications.
It supports all major fast charging standards including USB PD 3.1 with PPS, Qualcomm QC 4.0+, Huawei SCP/FCP, and more.
The IC features automatic protocol detection, cable compensation, and comprehensive protection for safe and efficient charging.

specifications.Supported Protocols: PD 3.0/3.1 PPS, QC 2.0/3.0/4.0+, SCP, FCP, AFC, PE

features: USB PD 3.1 PPS support for precise power control
Comprehensive protection (OVP, UVP, OCP, SCP, OTP)

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Solutions
faeReview.content: In my experience supporting numerous charging designs, the IP2723T stands out for its exceptional protocol compatibility and ease of use. I highly recommend this IC for designers looking to create universal charging solutions. The automatic protocol detection eliminates complex software development, while the integrated protections reduce external component count. I've successfully implemented this in over 50 designs, and the reliability has been excellent. For power bank applications, the low standby current is particularly valuable for extending battery life. The I2C interface provides flexibility for advanced features while the GPIO options suit simpler designs.
faeReview.highlight: Universal protocol support with excellent integration

alternativeParts.comparison: IP2723T=><Cypress CYPD3177: Package QFN-24 = QFN-24 (same), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Injoinic IP2723T offers broader protocol support including QC, SCP, FCP while maintaining similar PD capabilities
alternativeParts.useCase: Use IP2723T when multi-protocol compatibility is required; CYPD3177 for PD-only applications

alternativeParts.comparison: IP2723T=><FP6606: Output current 5A > 3A (+67%, higher current), Package QFN-24 vs SOP-8 (different), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: IP2723T supports higher voltage and current with more protocols including PD
alternativeParts.useCase: IP2723T for high-power PD applications; FP6606 for basic QC applications

companionParts.description: Synchronous buck-boost converter for power bank applications
Dual-port fast charging controller for multi-port designs
USB Type-C receptacle with CC pins for PD communication
Low Rds(on) MOSFET for power path switching

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power supported by IP2723T?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T supports up to 100W output power (20V/5A) when configured for USB PD applications. The actual maximum power depends on the input power source and power stage design. For optimal performance, ensure your power stage can support the desired output current with adequate thermal management. The IC handles protocol negotiation up to the configured limits.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 100W applications, ensure proper power stage design and thermal management. Contact us for reference designs.

faqs.question: How do I update the firmware in IP2723T?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T supports firmware updates through the I2C interface. A host MCU can write new firmware to the internal flash memory. The update procedure requires entering bootloader mode, erasing the existing firmware, and programming the new version. Injoinic provides firmware update tools and documentation. Always follow the recommended procedure to avoid bricking the device.
faqs.decisionGuide: For firmware updates, implement a reliable update mechanism in your host MCU. Contact Injoinic for firmware update tools.

faqs.question: What is the typical application circuit for IP2723T?
faqs.answer: The typical application requires the IP2723T connected to a Type-C connector with CC pins, external MOSFETs for power path control, and current sense resistors. The IC interfaces with a DC-DC converter or power stage through feedback pins. External components include decoupling capacitors, pull-up/down resistors for configuration, and optional I2C pull-ups. Reference schematics are available showing complete power bank and adapter designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from our team for your specific application. We provide complete schematics and BOMs.

faqs.question: Does IP2723T support dual-port applications?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T is designed for single-port applications. For dual-port designs, consider the IP2726 which supports two independent ports with intelligent power allocation. The IP2726 can dynamically distribute power between ports based on connected devices and their charging requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IP2723T for single-port designs. Select IP2726 for dual-port applications requiring intelligent power sharing.

faqs.question: What is the standby power consumption of IP2723T?
faqs.answer: The IP2723T features ultra-low standby power consumption, typically less than 100μA when no device is connected. This low standby current is crucial for power bank applications to maximize battery life. The IC automatically enters low-power mode when idle and wakes up when a device is detected.
faqs.decisionGuide: The low standby current makes IP2723T ideal for battery-powered applications. Contact us for detailed power consumption data.

faqs.question: How do I troubleshoot protocol detection issues?
faqs.answer: If the IP2723T fails to detect protocols, check the CC pin connections and pull-up resistors. Verify the Type-C cable is properly connected and supports the required current. Use a protocol analyzer to monitor CC line communication. Check that the IC is properly powered and not in fault mode. Review the configuration pins for correct voltage/current limit settings. Our FAE team can provide detailed troubleshooting guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For protocol detection issues, verify hardware connections and configuration. Contact our FAE team for troubleshooting support.

products.name: Dual-Port Fast Charging Controller
products.shortDescription: Dual-port multi-protocol fast charging IC with intelligent power allocation, supporting simultaneous fast charging of...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP2726 is a dual-port fast charging controller designed for multi-port chargers and power banks.
It supports intelligent power allocation between two ports, optimizing charging performance for connected devices.
The IC supports all major fast charging protocols and can deliver up to 100W total output power.

specifications.Supported Protocols: PD 3.0/3.1 PPS, QC 2.0/3.0/4.0+, SCP, FCP, AFC, PE

faeReview.author: Michael Wang
faeReview.title: FAE Manager - Consumer Electronics
faeReview.content: The IP2726 is my go-to recommendation for dual-port charging designs. The intelligent power allocation feature is particularly impressive - it automatically optimizes power distribution based on each device's capabilities and requirements. I've implemented this in several high-volume products, and the performance has been consistently excellent. The ability to deliver 65W on one port while maintaining fast charging on the second port sets it apart from competitors. For designers creating multi-port chargers, this IC significantly simplifies the design while providing superior user experience.
faeReview.highlight: Intelligent dual-port power allocation up to 100W total

alternativeParts.reason: IP2726 offers dual-port capability with intelligent allocation versus single-port CYPD3171
alternativeParts.useCase: IP2726 for dual-port designs; CYPD3171 for single high-power PD applications

alternativeParts.reason: IP2726 supports higher power and more protocols including PD 3.1 PPS
alternativeParts.useCase: IP2726 for high-power multi-protocol designs; SW3518 for basic dual-port QC

companionParts.description: Synchronous buck-boost converter for power stage
Single-port controller for expansion designs
Dual USB Type-C receptacles for two ports
Precision resistors for current monitoring

faqs.question: How does the intelligent power allocation work in IP2726?
faqs.answer: The IP2726 monitors the power requirements of devices connected to both ports and dynamically allocates available power. When a single device is connected, it can receive up to 100W. When two devices are connected, the IC intelligently splits power based on each device's capabilities and charging profile. For example, it might allocate 65W to a laptop and 30W to a phone, or 45W to each if both devices support similar power levels. The allocation updates in real-time as device requirements change.
faqs.decisionGuide: The intelligent allocation optimizes charging for multiple devices. Contact us for detailed power allocation algorithms and configuration options.

faqs.question: Can both ports support fast charging simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Yes, both ports support fast charging simultaneously with intelligent power sharing. The total output power is limited to 100W, so the specific power per port depends on the connected devices and their requirements. Both ports support all major fast charging protocols, ensuring optimal charging performance regardless of device combination.
faqs.decisionGuide: Both ports support fast charging. Design your power stage to handle the total 100W output with adequate thermal management.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IP2726 and using two IP2723T chips?
faqs.answer: The IP2726 integrates dual-port control with intelligent power management in a single chip, reducing BOM cost and PCB space. Using two IP2723T chips requires external power management logic to coordinate between ports. The IP2726's built-in intelligence ensures optimal power allocation without external MCU intervention. For cost-sensitive designs with limited space, IP2726 is the preferred solution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IP2726 for integrated dual-port solutions. Consider dual IP2723T only if ports need to be physically separated or for specific architectural requirements.

faqs.question: How do I configure power allocation limits?
faqs.answer: Power allocation can be configured through the I2C interface or external resistors. You can set maximum power limits for each port and define allocation priorities. The IC respects these limits while optimizing power distribution within the constraints. For example, you can limit Port 1 to 65W maximum and Port 2 to 35W, or configure equal 50W limits for both ports.
faqs.decisionGuide: Configure power limits based on your power stage capabilities and thermal design. Our FAE team can provide configuration guidance.

faqs.question: Does IP2726 support USB-C source mode (DRP)?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IP2726 supports Dual Role Power (DRP) operation, allowing the port to function as either a source (provider) or sink (consumer). This is useful for applications like power banks that need to both charge internal batteries and power external devices. The IC handles role negotiation automatically through the USB PD protocol.
faqs.decisionGuide: For DRP applications like power banks, IP2726 provides flexible source/sink operation. Contact us for DRP reference designs.

faqs.question: What thermal considerations are needed for IP2726 designs?
faqs.answer: At 100W total output, thermal management is critical. The IP2726 itself has low power dissipation, but the external power stage components require proper heat sinking. Consider using synchronous rectification for high efficiency, adequate copper area for heat spreading, and thermal vias to inner ground planes. For enclosed designs, ensure sufficient airflow or add thermal management features.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for thermal management at maximum power. We can provide thermal design guidelines and reference layouts.

The IC offers a simplified feature set focused on essential PD functionality.

The IC supports all EPR voltage levels up to 28V.

The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: High-efficiency power management solutions including DC-DC converters, LDOs, and PMICs for mobile devices and consumer electronics.
categories.longDescription: Injoinic's power management ICs provide efficient power conversion and regulation for consumer electronics and mobile devices. The product range includes synchronous buck-boost converters, step-down regulators, and multi-channel PMICs. These ICs feature high efficiency across load ranges, fast transient response, and comprehensive protection features. With compact packages and minimal external components, Injoinic PMICs enable space-efficient designs for smartphones, tablets, TWS earphones, and IoT devices. The high integration level reduces BOM cost while maintaining excellent performance.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Power Management ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider input/output voltage, current requirements, efficiency, and package size

faqs.question: What types of DC-DC converters does Injoinic offer?
faqs.answer: Injoinic offers synchronous buck, boost, and buck-boost converters for various applications. Buck converters step down voltage efficiently for powering low-voltage circuits. Boost converters step up voltage for applications requiring higher output than input. Buck-boost converters handle both step-up and step-down operation, ideal for battery-powered applications where input voltage varies above and below the output. All feature synchronous rectification for high efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select converter topology based on your input/output voltage relationship. Contact us for topology selection guidance.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of Injoinic DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Injoinic DC-DC converters achieve high efficiency, typically 90-95% at moderate to heavy loads. Efficiency varies with input voltage, output voltage, and load current. Synchronous rectification eliminates diode losses, improving efficiency especially at low output voltages. Light-load efficiency is optimized through features like automatic PFM mode switching. Refer to efficiency curves in datasheets for specific operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, efficiency directly impacts runtime. Review efficiency curves for your operating conditions.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in Injoinic PMICs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic PMICs include comprehensive protection features: over-current protection (OCP) prevents damage from excessive load current; over-voltage protection (OVP) safeguards against input voltage spikes; under-voltage lockout (UVLO) ensures proper startup; over-temperature protection (OTP) prevents thermal damage; and short-circuit protection (SCP) handles output faults. These protections ensure reliable operation and protect both the IC and connected circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated protections simplify design and improve reliability. Review datasheet for specific protection thresholds.

faqs.question: How do I select the right inductor for Injoinic DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Inductor selection affects efficiency, ripple current, and transient response. Choose inductance value based on switching frequency and desired ripple current (typically 20-40% of output current). Higher inductance reduces ripple but may slow transient response. Consider saturation current rating (should exceed maximum load plus ripple) and DCR (affects efficiency). Shielded inductors reduce EMI. Injoinic datasheets provide recommended inductor values and part numbers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet recommendations for inductor selection. Contact our FAE team for optimization guidance.

faqs.question: What package options are available for Injoinic PMICs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic PMICs are available in compact packages suitable for space-constrained applications. Common packages include QFN (2x2mm to 5x5mm), SOT23, and DFN. The small packages enable high power density designs for smartphones, wearables, and IoT devices. Thermal performance varies by package, with larger packages offering better heat dissipation. Select package based on space constraints, thermal requirements, and manufacturing considerations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose package based on space and thermal requirements. Review PCB layout guidelines in datasheets.

products.name: Synchronous Buck-Boost Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency synchronous buck-boost converter with wide input range, ideal for power bank and battery-powered appl...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP6538 is a high-efficiency synchronous buck-boost converter designed for battery-powered applications.
It seamlessly transitions between buck and boost modes as input voltage varies above and below the output.
The IC delivers up to 18W output power with excellent efficiency across the entire battery discharge curve.

faeReview.author: Lisa Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The IP6538 is an excellent choice for power bank designs requiring wide input range and high efficiency. I particularly appreciate the seamless mode transition - there's no glitch or instability when crossing the buck-boost boundary. In my designs, I've achieved consistent 93-96% efficiency across the entire lithium battery voltage range. The integrated MOSFETs reduce BOM cost and PCB space significantly. For designers creating power banks or battery-powered products, this IC provides a reliable, efficient solution with minimal external components.

alternativeParts.reason: IP6538 supports wider voltage range and higher output voltage than TPS63020
alternativeParts.useCase: IP6538 for wide-range applications; TPS63020 for low-voltage designs

alternativeParts.comparison: IP6538=><LTC3780: Package QFN-24 < TSSOP-24 (smaller), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: IP6538 offers higher integration in smaller package for lower power applications
alternativeParts.useCase: IP6538 for compact sub-20W designs; LTC3780 for high-power applications

companionParts.description: Protocol IC for USB PD fast charging applications
Battery protection IC for lithium battery packs
Shielded inductor for buck-boost converter
Low ESR ceramic capacitors for input/output filtering

faqs.question: How does the buck-boost transition work in IP6538?
faqs.answer: The IP6538 uses a seamless transition topology that eliminates mode-switching glitches. When input voltage is higher than output, it operates in buck mode. When input drops below output, it automatically transitions to boost mode. The transition is smooth with no output voltage disturbance. This is achieved through a 4-switch H-bridge topology that reconfigures based on input/output relationship.
faqs.decisionGuide: The seamless transition ensures stable output regardless of battery voltage. Ideal for applications using lithium batteries with voltage ranging from 4.2V to 3.0V.

faqs.question: What is the recommended PCB layout for IP6538?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is critical for stable operation. Place input and output capacitors close to the IC with short, wide traces. Use a solid ground plane under the IC and connect all ground pins directly to it. Route the switching node away from sensitive signals. Ensure adequate copper area for heat dissipation, especially for the exposed pad. Follow the layout guidelines in the datasheet for optimal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow the reference layout in the datasheet. Contact us for layout review services.

faqs.question: Can IP6538 be used for USB PD applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, IP6538 is ideal for USB PD power bank applications. It can work with protocol ICs like IP2723T to create complete PD solutions. The wide input range accommodates lithium battery voltage variation (3.0V-4.2V), while the output can be configured for 5V, 9V, 12V, or other PD voltage levels. The high efficiency maximizes battery runtime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Combine IP6538 with IP2723T for complete USB PD power bank solutions. Reference designs are available.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency of IP6538?
faqs.answer: The IP6538 operates at a fixed 500kHz switching frequency. This frequency provides a good balance between efficiency, component size, and EMI. The frequency is high enough to allow small inductors and capacitors, yet low enough to maintain good efficiency and minimize switching losses. The fixed frequency simplifies EMI filtering design.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 500kHz switching frequency suits most applications. Consider EMI requirements when designing the PCB.

faqs.question: How do I set the output voltage?
faqs.answer: Output voltage is set using external resistor dividers connected to the FB pin. The feedback reference voltage is typically 0.6V or 1.0V depending on configuration. Calculate resistor values using the formula: Vout = Vref × (1 + R1/R2). The datasheet provides detailed calculations and recommended values for common output voltages. Some variants support I2C voltage adjustment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use resistor dividers for fixed outputs, I2C for dynamic adjustment. Contact us for configuration assistance.

faqs.question: What is the quiescent current of IP6538?
faqs.answer: The IP6538 features low quiescent current, typically less than 100μA in standby mode. This low current consumption is important for battery-powered applications to maximize standby time. When the converter is active, the operating current increases with load, but efficiency remains high across the load range.
faqs.decisionGuide: The low quiescent current makes IP6538 suitable for battery-powered applications. Review power consumption specs in the datasheet.

products.name: Power Bank SOC
products.shortDescription: Highly integrated power bank system-on-chip combining boost converter, battery management, and LED indicators in a si...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP5306 is a highly integrated power bank SOC that combines multiple functions in a single chip.
It integrates a synchronous boost converter, battery charging, power path management, and LED indicators.
This high integration reduces BOM cost and simplifies PCB design for power bank applications.

faeReview.author: Kevin Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Consumer Power Solutions
faeReview.content: The IP5306 is perfect for entry-level power bank designs where cost and simplicity are priorities. The integration level is impressive - you get charging, boost conversion, and LED indicators in one chip. I've helped customers create complete power bank designs with fewer than 15 components. The efficiency is good for this integration level, and the protection features are comprehensive. For basic 5V/2A power banks, this IC offers unbeatable value. It's not for high-power PD applications, but for standard power banks, it's an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: All-in-one power bank solution with minimal BOM

alternativeParts.comparison: IP5306=><SW6106: Package ESOP-8 = ESOP-8 (same), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Both offer similar integration; IP5306 has proven reliability in mass production
alternativeParts.useCase: IP5306 for proven designs; SW6106 for higher output current needs

alternativeParts.reason: IP5306 offers higher power and more integration than basic alternatives
alternativeParts.useCase: IP5306 for feature-rich designs; TP5400 for very basic applications

companionParts.description: 18650 or polymer lithium battery cell
USB-A and Micro-USB connectors
Battery level indicator LEDs
Power inductor for boost converter

faqs.question: What battery types does IP5306 support?
faqs.answer: The IP5306 is designed for single-cell lithium-ion or lithium-polymer batteries with nominal voltage of 3.7V. It supports batteries with capacity from 1000mAh to 10000mAh or more. The charging voltage is fixed at 4.2V, suitable for standard lithium cells. For other battery chemistries or configurations, consider other solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IP5306 with standard 3.7V lithium cells. Contact us for other battery configurations.

faqs.question: How does the LED indicator work?
faqs.answer: The IP5306 drives 4 LEDs to indicate battery level. Each LED represents approximately 25% of battery capacity. During charging, the LEDs light up sequentially to show progress. When discharging, the LEDs indicate remaining capacity. The LED control is automatic and requires no external MCU.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 4-LED indicator provides clear battery status. Connect LEDs as shown in the reference schematic.

faqs.question: Can IP5306 support pass-through charging?
faqs.answer: Yes, IP5306 supports pass-through charging where the output remains active while charging the battery. The IC intelligently manages power flow, prioritizing output while charging the battery with available current. This allows the power bank to function as a UPS-style device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Pass-through charging is supported automatically. No special configuration needed.

faqs.question: What is the maximum battery capacity supported?
faqs.answer: There is no specific maximum battery capacity limit for IP5306. The IC can work with any capacity lithium cell from 1000mAh to 20000mAh or more. Charging time will vary based on battery capacity - larger batteries take longer to charge at the fixed 2A charging current. The discharge time depends on load and battery capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select battery capacity based on your product requirements. Charging time = Capacity / 2A.

faqs.question: Does IP5306 support fast charging protocols?
faqs.answer: The IP5306 provides standard 5V/2.1A output without protocol negotiation. For fast charging with QC, PD, or other protocols, pair IP5306 with a protocol IC like IP2723T. The protocol IC handles device negotiation while IP5306 provides the power conversion.
faqs.decisionGuide: For standard 5V power banks, IP5306 works standalone. For fast charging, add IP2723T protocol IC.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the power bank conversion efficiency?
faqs.answer: Power bank efficiency considers both charging and discharging. Charging efficiency is typically 85-90% (battery energy / input energy). Discharging efficiency is 90-93% (output energy / battery energy). Overall efficiency is approximately 75-80%. IP5306 achieves good efficiency through synchronous rectification in both directions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 75-80% overall efficiency. This is typical for power bank applications.

It features a wide input voltage range and adjustable output voltage.
The IC is ideal for powering MCUs and digital circuits in charging applications.

categories.name: Wireless Charging ICs
categories.description: Wireless power transmission solutions compliant with Qi standard for transmitter and receiver applications.
categories.longDescription: Injoinic's wireless charging ICs provide complete solutions for Qi-compatible wireless power applications. The product range includes transmitter ICs for charging pads and receiver ICs for device integration. These ICs support various power levels from 5W basic charging to 15W fast wireless charging. Features include foreign object detection (FOD), temperature monitoring, and comprehensive protection. The high integration reduces external component count, enabling compact and cost-effective wireless charging solutions for smartphones, TWS earphones, and accessories.

series.name: IP Series TX
series.description: Wireless power transmitter ICs for charging pads

series.name: IP Series RX
series.description: Wireless power receiver ICs for device integration

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Wireless Charging ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider power level, coil configuration, and features for your wireless charging application

faqs.question: What Qi power levels does Injoinic support?
faqs.answer: Injoinic wireless charging ICs support multiple Qi power levels. The 5W baseline power profile (BPP) is supported for basic charging. The 7.5W and 10W extended power profiles (EPP) enable faster charging. Some models support up to 15W for premium fast wireless charging. The specific power level depends on the IC model and coil design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select IC based on required charging speed. 5W for basic, 10-15W for fast wireless charging.

faqs.question: What is foreign object detection (FOD) in wireless charging?
faqs.answer: Foreign Object Detection (FOD) is a safety feature that detects metallic objects placed between the transmitter and receiver. The IC monitors power transfer efficiency and temperature. If a foreign object is detected, charging is stopped to prevent heating and potential damage. Injoinic ICs include advanced FOD algorithms for reliable detection.
faqs.decisionGuide: FOD is essential for safety certification. Injoinic ICs include robust FOD features.

faqs.question: What coil configurations are supported?
faqs.answer: Injoinic transmitter ICs support various coil configurations including single coil, multi-coil arrays, and free-position designs. Single coil is simplest and most cost-effective. Multi-coil arrays allow charging in different positions. The coil inductance typically ranges from 6μH to 24μH depending on the design. Receiver ICs work with standard receiver coils.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select coil configuration based on positioning requirements. Contact us for coil design guidance.

faqs.question: How do I get Qi certification for my wireless charging product?
faqs.answer: Qi certification requires testing by a WPC authorized test lab. The process includes compliance testing against Qi specifications and interoperability testing with other Qi devices. Injoinic's Qi-compliant ICs simplify certification. You'll need to submit your product for testing and pay certification fees. LiTong can provide guidance on the certification process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Qi-compliant Injoinic ICs and follow WPC guidelines. Contact us for certification support.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of Injoinic wireless charging solutions?
faqs.answer: Wireless charging efficiency depends on coil design, alignment, and power level. Typical end-to-end efficiency ranges from 70-80% for well-designed systems. The transmitter IC efficiency is typically 90%+, with additional losses in the coils and receiver. Injoinic ICs optimize efficiency through advanced power control and modulation techniques.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 70-80% overall efficiency. Proper coil design and alignment are critical.

products.name: Wireless Power Transmitter IC
products.shortDescription: Qi-compliant wireless power transmitter IC supporting up to 15W fast wireless charging with integrated drivers and ad...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP6808 is a Qi-compliant wireless power transmitter IC for charging pad applications.
It supports up to 15W fast wireless charging with automatic power adjustment based on receiver requirements.
The IC integrates full-bridge drivers, power control, and communication protocols for a complete solution.

faeReview.author: Alex Huang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Power
faeReview.content: The IP6808 is a solid choice for wireless charging pad designs. The 15W capability enables fast charging for modern smartphones. I find the FOD detection particularly reliable - it correctly identifies foreign objects without false triggers. The integrated drivers reduce component count significantly. In my experience, designs using IP6808 pass Qi certification smoothly. The power efficiency is good for the price point. For designers entering the wireless charging market, this IC provides a cost-effective path to Qi-certified products.
faeReview.highlight: Qi-certified 15W wireless charging with reliable FOD

faqs.question: What is the maximum charging distance for IP6808?
faqs.answer: The typical charging distance for IP6808 is 3-8mm between transmitter and receiver coils. This accommodates most phone cases and accessories. The actual range depends on coil size, alignment, and any materials between the coils. For best performance, maintain close coupling between coils.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 3-8mm typical gap. Thicker cases may reduce efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I design the transmitter coil for IP6808?
faqs.answer: Transmitter coil design follows Qi specifications. Use 6.3μH inductance with ±10% tolerance for A11 coils. The coil typically has 10-15 turns of Litz wire for optimal high-frequency performance. Coil diameter should be 50mm for standard applications. Follow WPC coil design guidelines for certification compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard A11 coil design for Qi compliance. Contact us for coil supplier recommendations.

faqs.question: Does IP6808 support multi-device charging?
faqs.answer: The IP6808 is designed for single-device charging. For multi-device charging pads, multiple IP6808 chips can be used with independent coils, or consider multi-coil designs with appropriate control logic. Each coil requires its own transmitter IC for independent power control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use one IP6808 per charging position. For multi-device pads, use multiple ICs.

faqs.question: What safety certifications are required for wireless chargers?
faqs.answer: Wireless chargers typically require Qi certification from WPC for interoperability. Safety certifications include CE (Europe), FCC (USA), UL (safety), and possibly others depending on target markets. The IP6808's Qi compliance simplifies certification. Additional testing may be required for thermal safety and EMC compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for Qi certification and regional safety certifications. We can provide certification guidance.

faqs.question: How does the IP6808 communicate with the receiver?
faqs.answer: The IP6808 uses the Qi communication protocol, which modulates the power carrier frequency to send data from receiver to transmitter. The receiver sends packets containing power control information, identification, and error data. The transmitter demodulates this information and adjusts power accordingly. This bidirectional communication enables safe and efficient power transfer.
faqs.decisionGuide: Communication is automatic per Qi standard. No additional configuration needed.

faqs.question: Can IP6808 work with any Qi-compatible receiver?
faqs.answer: Yes, IP6808 is Qi-compliant and works with any Qi-certified receiver device. This includes smartphones from Apple, Samsung, Google, and other manufacturers that support Qi charging. The IC automatically negotiates power levels based on the receiver's capabilities, ensuring compatibility across the Qi ecosystem.
faqs.decisionGuide: IP6808 works with all Qi devices. No compatibility concerns for standard Qi receivers.

products.name: Wireless Power Receiver IC
products.shortDescription: Qi-compliant wireless power receiver IC with integrated rectifier and voltage regulation for device-side wireless cha...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP6806 is a Qi-compliant wireless power receiver IC for integration into devices.
It includes a synchronous rectifier, voltage regulator, and communication controller in a single chip.
The IC delivers regulated output voltage for charging batteries or powering devices wirelessly.

faeReview.author: Sarah Wu
faeReview.title: FAE - Portable Electronics
faeReview.content: The IP6806 is ideal for adding wireless charging to compact devices. The WLCSP package is tiny - perfect for space-constrained designs like TWS earphone cases. The integration level is excellent with rectifier and regulator in one chip. I've used this in several wearable projects with good results. The 5W output is sufficient for small batteries. For larger devices, consider higher-power receiver solutions. The Qi compliance ensures compatibility with standard charging pads.
faeReview.highlight: Compact WLCSP receiver for small device integration

faqs.question: What coil should I use with IP6806?
faqs.answer: IP6806 works with standard Qi receiver coils, typically 10-15 turns with 10-15μH inductance. The coil size depends on your device dimensions - common sizes are 30-40mm diameter for smartphones, smaller for wearables. Use Litz wire for best high-frequency performance. The coil should be placed where it can couple effectively with transmitter coils.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Qi receiver coils. Match coil size to your device dimensions.

faqs.question: Can IP6806 charge a battery directly?
faqs.answer: IP6806 outputs regulated 5V, which can charge a battery through an external charger IC. For lithium batteries, use a dedicated battery charger IC between IP6806 and the battery. The 5V output can also power devices directly or supply a PMIC. Direct battery connection without proper charging control is not recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add a battery charger IC for lithium battery charging. Contact us for charger recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I position the receiver coil in my device?
faqs.answer: Position the receiver coil where it can align with typical transmitter coils. For smartphones, this is usually centered on the back. Avoid metal objects directly behind the coil - use ferrite shielding if necessary. Maintain specified distance from metal components. The coil should be accessible for magnetic coupling with charging pads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Center the coil on the device back. Use ferrite shielding if metal is behind the coil.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of IP6806?
faqs.answer: IP6806 has good thermal performance for its power level. At 5W output, the IC typically operates within safe temperature limits. Thermal performance depends on PCB copper area, ambient temperature, and charging duration. Use thermal vias under the chip and adequate copper area for heat spreading. The IC includes over-temperature protection for safety.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide adequate copper area for heat dissipation. The IC has built-in thermal protection.

faqs.question: Does IP6806 support higher power like 10W or 15W?
faqs.answer: The IP6806 is designed for 5W Baseline Power Profile. For higher power wireless charging (10W or 15W), consider other receiver solutions that support Extended Power Profile. Higher power requires different coil design, thermal management, and communication protocols. Contact us for high-power receiver recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: IP6806 is for 5W applications. Contact us for 10W or 15W receiver solutions.

faqs.question: How do I test the wireless charging function?
faqs.answer: Test wireless charging using a Qi-certified transmitter pad. Place your device on the pad and verify charging starts. Check output voltage with a multimeter. Monitor temperature during extended charging. Test with various transmitters to ensure compatibility. Use a USB power meter to measure actual power transfer. Qi test equipment can verify compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Qi-certified charging pads for testing. Verify with multiple transmitter brands.

categories.name: Battery Management ICs
categories.description: Comprehensive battery protection and management solutions for lithium-ion and lithium-polymer batteries.
categories.longDescription: Injoinic's battery management ICs provide essential protection and monitoring functions for lithium battery packs. The product range includes single-cell and multi-cell protection ICs, battery monitors, and fuel gauges. These ICs protect against over-charge, over-discharge, over-current, and short-circuit conditions. Advanced features include cell balancing for multi-cell packs, temperature monitoring, and communication interfaces. The high integration and low power consumption make these ICs ideal for portable electronics, power banks, and battery-powered devices.

series.name: IP Series Protection
series.description: Single and multi-cell battery protection ICs

series.name: IP Series AFE
series.description: Analog front-end ICs for battery monitoring

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Battery Management ICs
selectionGuide.description: Consider cell count, protection requirements, and monitoring features for your battery pack

faqs.question: What protection functions do Injoinic battery management ICs provide?
faqs.answer: Injoinic battery protection ICs provide comprehensive safety functions: over-charge protection prevents battery over-voltage during charging; over-discharge protection prevents excessive discharge that could damage the battery; over-current protection limits discharge current to safe levels; short-circuit protection quickly disconnects the battery during fault conditions; and temperature protection monitors battery temperature for safe operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: All Injoinic battery ICs include essential protections. Select based on cell count and current requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right protection IC for my battery pack?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right protection IC involves several factors: cell count (single-cell or multi-cell), voltage thresholds (matching your battery chemistry), current ratings (exceeding your maximum load current), package size (fitting your PCB constraints), and additional features (balancing, temperature monitoring, etc.). Injoinic offers options for various configurations. Review datasheets for specific threshold voltages and timing parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match IC specifications to your battery and application requirements. Contact us for selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is cell balancing and when is it needed?
faqs.answer: Cell balancing equalizes voltage across series-connected cells in multi-cell battery packs. Over time, cells can develop different charge levels, reducing pack capacity and potentially causing over-charge/discharge of individual cells. Balancing circuits redistribute charge to maintain equal cell voltages. It's essential for multi-cell packs (2S and above) to maximize capacity and ensure safety. Injoinic offers protection ICs with integrated balancing functions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use balancing for 2S and higher cell counts. Injoinic multi-cell ICs include balancing features.

faqs.question: What is the difference between protection IC and fuel gauge IC?
faqs.answer: Protection ICs focus on safety, monitoring voltage, current, and temperature to disconnect the battery during fault conditions. Fuel gauge ICs estimate remaining battery capacity and provide state-of-charge information to the system. Some Injoinic ICs combine both functions, while others are dedicated to protection only. For complete battery management, you may need both functions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use protection ICs for safety. Add fuel gauge if accurate capacity reporting is needed.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the current sense resistor value?
faqs.answer: Current sense resistor value is calculated based on protection threshold and resistor power rating. Typical values range from 1mΩ to 10mΩ. Lower resistance reduces power loss but requires more sensitive detection. The formula is: R = Vthreshold / Iprotection. Ensure the resistor power rating exceeds I²R losses at maximum current. Consider using 1% tolerance resistors for accurate protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on protection current and threshold voltage. Use low-tolerance resistors for accuracy.

products.name: Single-Cell Battery Protection IC
products.shortDescription: Cost-effective single-cell lithium battery protection IC with over-charge, over-discharge, and over-current protectio...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP3005 is a single-cell lithium battery protection IC providing essential safety functions.
It monitors battery voltage and current, disconnecting the battery during fault conditions.
The compact SOT23-6 package and minimal external components make it ideal for space-constrained designs.
features: Over-discharge protection with low standby current

faeReview.author: Tom Li
faeReview.title: FAE - Battery Solutions
faeReview.content: The IP3005 is my standard recommendation for single-cell battery protection. It's cost-effective, reliable, and the SOT23-6 package fits almost anywhere. I've used this in countless designs from power banks to wearables. The protection thresholds are well-calibrated for lithium cells. The low standby current is crucial for battery life. For basic protection needs, this IC offers excellent value. It's a drop-in replacement for many popular protection ICs with better cost.

companionParts.description: Buck-boost converter for power bank applications
Power bank SOC with integrated charger
18650 or polymer lithium battery cell
Precision resistor for over-current detection

faqs.question: How does the IP3005 protection circuit work?
faqs.answer: IP3005 continuously monitors battery voltage and current. If voltage exceeds the over-charge threshold during charging, the IC disconnects the charge path. If voltage drops below the over-discharge threshold, it disconnects the discharge path. For over-current conditions, the IC detects excessive current flow and opens the discharge path. All protections have hysteresis to prevent oscillation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Protection is automatic. No external control needed. The IC protects the battery in all fault conditions.

faqs.question: What happens after a protection event?
faqs.answer: After over-charge protection, normal operation resumes when voltage drops below the release threshold (typically by connecting a load). After over-discharge protection, charging resumes when a charger is connected and voltage rises above the release threshold. Over-current protection typically latches and requires removing the load or charger to reset.
faqs.decisionGuide: Most protections auto-recover when fault is removed. Check datasheet for specific recovery conditions.

faqs.question: Can IP3005 be used with high-capacity battery cells?
faqs.answer: Yes, IP3005 works with any capacity single-cell lithium battery. The protection thresholds are voltage-based, not capacity-dependent. For high-capacity cells (5000mAh+), ensure the over-current threshold is set appropriately for your application's maximum current draw. The current sense resistor may need adjustment for very high current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: IP3005 works with all capacities. Adjust current protection for high-current applications.

faqs.question: What external components are needed with IP3005?
faqs.answer: IP3005 requires minimal external components: a current sense resistor for over-current detection, decoupling capacitors on power pins, and optionally a delay capacitor for timing adjustments. The internal MOSFETs eliminate the need for external switching components. This minimal BOM reduces cost and PCB space.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimal external components needed. Follow reference schematic in datasheet.

faqs.question: How do I select the current sense resistor?
faqs.answer: Calculate sense resistor using R = Vdetection / Itrip, where Vdetection is the over-current detection voltage (typically 100-200mV) and Itrip is your desired protection current. For example, for 3A protection with 150mV detection: R = 0.15V / 3A = 50mΩ. Ensure resistor power rating exceeds I²R losses (3A² × 0.05Ω = 0.45W, use 1W resistor).
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on desired trip current. Use adequate power rating. Contact us for calculation assistance.

faqs.question: Does IP3005 include temperature protection?
faqs.answer: The IP3005 focuses on voltage and current protection. For temperature protection, add an external NTC thermistor and monitoring circuit, or select a protection IC with integrated temperature monitoring. Some applications may use the battery pack's existing temperature protection instead.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add external temperature monitoring if needed. Contact us for temperature protection solutions.

products.name: 2-3S Battery Protection IC
products.shortDescription: Multi-cell battery protection IC supporting 2-3 series lithium cells with cell balancing and comprehensive protection...
descriptionParagraphs: The IP3008 is a multi-cell battery protection IC designed for 2-3 series lithium battery packs.
It provides individual cell monitoring, cell balancing, and comprehensive protection functions.
The IC is ideal for higher voltage applications such as power tools, e-bikes, and portable power stations.

faeReview.author: Robert Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The IP3008 is a solid choice for multi-cell battery packs. The integrated balancing is a key feature - it eliminates the need for separate balancing circuits. I've used this in several power tool and e-bike applications with good results. The protection thresholds are appropriate for lithium cells, and the current handling is sufficient for moderate power applications. For higher current needs, external MOSFETs can be added. The TSSOP package is manageable for most designs.

companionParts.description: External MOSFETs for high-current applications
Temperature sensor for thermal protection
18650 or 21700 lithium cells for pack assembly
Resistors for cell balancing circuit

faqs.question: How does cell balancing work in IP3008?
faqs.answer: IP3008 monitors each cell voltage during charging. When a cell reaches full charge before others, the IC activates a bypass resistor for that cell, allowing other cells to continue charging. This passive balancing continues until all cells reach the same voltage. The typical balancing current is 50mA, suitable for most applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balancing is automatic during charging. No external control needed. Ensure adequate PCB heat dissipation for balancing resistors.

faqs.question: Can IP3008 handle high discharge currents?
faqs.answer: IP3008 internal MOSFETs support moderate currents up to 10-15A continuous. For higher currents (20A+), use external MOSFETs controlled by the IP3008. The IC provides gate drive signals for external high-current switches. This allows scaling to high-power applications while maintaining protection functionality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use internal FETs for currents under 15A. Add external MOSFETs for higher current applications.

faqs.question: How do I wire the cell connections for IP3008?
faqs.answer: Connect each cell tap to the corresponding VC pin on IP3008: VC1 to first cell positive, VC2 to second cell positive, VC3 to third cell positive (for 3S). Connect battery negative to VSS. Use short, matched traces for cell sense connections to avoid measurement errors. Add bypass capacitors near the IC for stable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow the reference schematic for cell connections. Keep sense traces short and matched.

faqs.question: What is the balancing accuracy of IP3008?
faqs.answer: IP3008 typically achieves cell voltage matching within 50mV after balancing. The balancing starts when the highest cell exceeds the threshold voltage (typically 4.1V) and continues until cells are balanced or charging completes. The accuracy depends on cell characteristics, charging current, and balancing time available.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect ~50mV balancing accuracy. For tighter matching, consider active balancing solutions.

faqs.question: Does IP3008 support temperature monitoring?
faqs.answer: Yes, IP3008 includes temperature monitoring through an external NTC thermistor. Connect the NTC between the TS pin and ground. The IC monitors temperature and can disable charging or discharging if temperature exceeds safe limits. This protects the battery from damage due to extreme temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add NTC thermistor for temperature protection. Follow datasheet for thermistor selection.

faqs.question: Can I use IP3008 for 4S or higher cell counts?
faqs.answer: IP3008 is designed for 2-3S configurations. For 4S or higher cell counts, consider other protection ICs designed for more cells, or use multiple protection ICs with appropriate configuration. Contact us for recommendations for higher cell count applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: IP3008 is for 2-3S only. Contact us for 4S+ protection solutions.
